role: IT Scrum Master / Agile Team Coach
subtitle: Team Dynamics · Coaching · AI-Enhanced Facilitation
hero_sub: I build trust quickly and hold a high accountability bar simultaneously. That combination is rarer than it sounds.

the_case.need: Proven experience improving team dynamics in low-accountability or high-friction environments
the_case.answer: Distressed program recovery at Understood.org. Cultural transformation at A&E (Post-its to Jira). Dysfunction remediation at Eptura. Resistance navigation at Underline.
the_case.detail: I've rebuilt team health in environments where trust was broken, accountability was absent, and delivery was stalling. At Understood.org I took over a distressed program in its final months and restored both team confidence and delivery discipline simultaneously. That's the hardest version of this job.

the_case.need: Demonstrated AI ability — as a thought partner and capability multiplier
the_case.answer: Jira AI + Copilot at Crocs (~30% overhead reduction). Six production AI-assisted applications shipped. AI embedded in planning, coaching, and reporting workflows across multiple engagements.
the_case.detail: I use AI to get sharper, faster insight from delivery data — not to automate the human parts of coaching. At Crocs I used Jira AI to surface patterns in backlog health and retrospective themes I would have caught later manually. That's the multiplier effect: more coaching time, less administrative time.

the_case.need: Ability to support 3–7 Scrum teams simultaneously
the_case.answer: Eptura: ~10 teams simultaneously. A&E: ~10 concurrent workstreams. Paperless Post: 2 teams. DigitasLBI: 2 teams. Multi-team coordination is the standard, not the exception.
the_case.detail: Supporting multiple teams isn't about splitting time evenly — it's about reading which team needs what at which moment. I've supported up to 10 teams simultaneously while maintaining individual coaching relationships with each Product Owner and team lead.

the_case.need: Address negative behaviors directly and constructively
the_case.answer: Direct, specific, non-judgmental feedback delivered immediately — not deferred to the next scheduled event.
the_case.detail: I don't let negative patterns run for a sprint before addressing them. When I see accountability gaps, communication breakdowns, or dysfunctional dynamics, I name them clearly and early. Not in public, not as criticism — as a coaching conversation with a specific ask.

the_case.need: Coach teams and stakeholders on Agile principles and ways of working
the_case.answer: 10+ years coaching engineers, Product Owners, and leadership on Agile principles. NEXUS Scrum coaching at Paperless Post. Agile adoption from scratch at Underline, Eptura Marketing, and A&E.
the_case.detail: I adapt coaching to maturity level. A team that has never run a retrospective needs different coaching than a team that's been doing Scrum for two years but has stopped improving. I assess first, then coach — not the other way around.

the_case.need: Use AI to enhance team effectiveness, insight, and decision-making
the_case.answer: Active daily user of Copilot, Jira AI, and Claude in delivery and coaching workflows — not aspirationally, but operationally.
the_case.detail: The AI tools requirement in this posting is real and specific — and most SM candidates can't satisfy it on paper. I use these tools actively: for planning synthesis, retrospective theme analysis, stakeholder communication, and coaching conversation preparation.

the_stack.headline: The coaching ecosystem.
the_stack.subhead: The tools and frameworks I use to coach teams toward high performance.

nodes.label: Jira & Jira AI
nodes.desc: Primary delivery tracking and AI-assisted backlog synthesis, sprint summaries, and pattern detection
nodes.pm_role: Maintain board health, configure workflows, and use Jira AI to surface delivery patterns that inform coaching interventions.

nodes.label: Azure DevOps (ADO)
nodes.desc: Enterprise delivery tracking at Eptura, Understood.org, and Crocs
nodes.pm_role: Board configuration, sprint dashboards, and team health metrics — used to identify where coaching energy should be focused.

nodes.label: Microsoft Copilot
nodes.desc: AI-assisted stakeholder communication, meeting synthesis, and administrative overhead reduction
nodes.pm_role: Frees coaching time by automating the administrative layer — status updates, decision logs, retrospective summaries.

nodes.label: Claude (AI)
nodes.desc: AI thought partner for coaching preparation, delivery analysis, and complex problem synthesis
nodes.pm_role: Used to prepare for difficult coaching conversations, analyze delivery data patterns, and synthesize retrospective themes across multiple teams.

nodes.label: Confluence & Smartsheet
nodes.desc: Team agreements, process documentation, and health tracking
nodes.pm_role: Working agreements, Definition of Done, team health frameworks, and continuous improvement tracking across teams.

when_things_break.scenario: Team has high velocity but low morale — delivering but not thriving
response: Separate the performance conversation from the health conversation
One-on-ones with each team member — not a group survey
Identify whether the issue is workload, recognition, collaboration, or leadership
Facilitate a team health retrospective with explicit psychological safety framing
Bring findings to leadership with specific, actionable recommendations — not just a diagnosis

when_things_break.scenario: Product Owner and Engineering Lead are misaligned on priorities — visible tension in ceremonies
response: Address it before the next sprint planning — not during it
Separate conversation with each to understand the real disconnect
Facilitate a structured prioritization session with explicit trade-off framework
Document the agreed decision and who owns it
Use the next retrospective to examine the process that allowed the misalignment to develop

when_things_break.scenario: Team is going through the motions — ceremonies happening but not producing anything
response: Name the pattern explicitly: 'We've been doing this for N sprints and nothing is changing'
Change the format completely for one sprint — different facilitation, different structure
Identify what's suppressing honest engagement: safety, relevance, or habit
Introduce a concrete, small experiment — one change, measurable in one sprint
If the team can't identify what needs to change, that IS the diagnosis to bring to leadership

how_i_run_a_room: Ceremonies produce decisions, not just discussion — I hold the room to that standard
Retrospectives generate at least one measurable workflow change per sprint
Difficult conversations happen in the moment — not at the next scheduled retrospective
Team health is assessed continuously, not annually — I know the state of each team
AI tools reduce my administrative load so I can spend more time coaching
Impediments have owners and deadlines within 24 hours of surfacing
I build trust by being consistent, direct, and following through — every time

points: Coached ~10 distributed teams through behavioral and cultural transformation
Spearheaded Agile adoption in Marketing department — zero prior Agile practice
35%+ delivery efficiency gain; 20% sprint carryover reduction
Mentored junior Scrum Masters; built org-wide Agile coaching framework
Rebuilt team health and delivery discipline in a distressed, high-friction program
Restored stakeholder confidence while simultaneously accelerating delivery
On-time replatform launch with no critical regressions after taking over mid-program
Operated in regulated, healthcare-adjacent environment with real compliance stakes
Led cultural transformation across ~10 teams over 2 years
Coached teams from ad-hoc manual delivery to self-organizing Agile execution
Coached Product Owners on backlog accountability and sprint discipline
Shipped all SVOD platforms while building team maturity simultaneously
Sold Scrum to a skeptical leadership team that had never used Agile
Coached through full adoption arc — initial resistance to confident delivery
Enterprise SaaS platform delivered in 16 weeks, on time and under budget

before: Ceremonies are happening but team dynamics are getting worse, not better
You're handling team conflict because the SM isn't addressing it
AI tools are mentioned in job descriptions but nobody on the team actually uses them in delivery
High performers are leaving because the team environment is draining them
after: Team dynamics improve measurably over 60 days — tracked, not assumed
Conflict surfaces and resolves at the team level before it reaches you
AI tools are embedded in actual delivery workflows, reducing overhead and sharpening insight
The team becomes a place people want to work — retention improves as a downstream effect

the_ramp.deliverable: Map real team dynamics across all 3–7 teams. One-on-ones with each team lead and PO. Identify the top accountability and health gaps before facilitating anything.
Facilitate first full ceremony cycle. Introduce one team health framework. Begin direct coaching conversations where needed.
First retrospective with measurable output. AI tools embedded in at least one workflow. Team health baseline documented.
At least one team dynamic measurably improved. Leadership has a clear, evidence-based picture of team health across all teams.
